preamble: The shift from the alveolar trill [r] to the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] is one of the most famous examples of areal diffusion and prestige-driven sound change in Europe. Known as the 'Guttural R' shift, it originated in 17th-century Paris and spread across much of Western Europe, replacing the apical trill in standard French, German, and Danish.

examples.note: The Parisian prestige pronunciation shifted the rhotic from the tongue-tip to the uvula during the 17th and 18th centuries.
German adapted the uvular R from French influence, starting in urban centers and spreading to the standard language.
